brief_justification: Paper A is an econometric factor-model diagnostic: a cumulative 'bridge-alpha' curve indexed by market capitalization plus HAC-Gaussian functional hypothesis testing to localize pricing errors in CRSP data. Paper B is a quantum-dynamics mechanism: subspace restart of a discrete-time quantum walk fragmenting the wavefunction (Huygens-Fresnel) to yield emergent drift-diffusion and a quantum-to-classical crossover. The only overlap is generic Gaussian/diffusion vocabulary, which plays incompatible roles (a null-distribution inference tool in A versus emergent physical transport in B), so no role-for-role machinery mapping holds.
